reason: HTTP is well-known port 80 (25=SMTP, 21=FTP).
MAC address is called both the hardware address and the physical address; option C ('both a and b') covers them.
A gateway is by definition a network point that acts as an entrance to another network.
UDP is connectionless with no acknowledgements/retransmission, i.e. unreliable.
Stream sockets (TCP) are full-duplex, providing two-way communication.
IEEE 802.5 Token Ring is a LAN data-link technology.
TELNET runs over TCP (port 23) for its reliable data connection.
URL = Uniform Resource Locator (RFC 1738).
JK excitation table: transition Q 0->1 requires J=1, K=don't-care.
JK/SR excitation table: transition Q 0->0 requires J(S)=0, K(R)=don't-care.
ROM is non-volatile; RAM/DIMM lose contents at power-off and CPU is not storage.
A network confined to a small geographic area is a Local Area Network.
The four-part dotted-quad identifier is the IPv4 address.
ENIAC = Electronic Numerical Integrator and Computer.
Binary convention: 1 KB = 1024 bytes; 1 MB = 1024 KB, so B, C, D are all wrong.
PNG (Portable Network Graphics) is a raster image format.
USB = Universal Serial Bus.
VoIP (Voice over IP) carries telephone calls over packet networks like the Internet.

Covertly running in the background and transmitting browsing habits to a third party is the textbook definition of spyware; grayware is only a loose umbrella term for unwanted software, and adware's defining behaviour is displaying ads.
Microsoft Windows is system software (an OS); Word/Excel/PowerPoint are applications.
